document_info.title: U14 Boys Costa Rica Tour - Training Session 2

session_practices.name: Technical Warm-Up: High-Tempo Combination, Turn & Move

pitch_diagram.image_description: A square 25x25 meter training grid. Four corner cones are labeled A, B, C, and D. In the exact center of the grid, a smaller 4x4 meter box is marked out using tall yellow cones to simulate a heavily congested central midfield zone.

tactical_layout.dimensions: 25m x 25m outer grid, 4m x 4m central zone
tactical_layout.equipment: 4 tall yellow cones forming the inner constraint
tactical_layout.stations: 4 corner positions executing continuous diagonal passing patterns

setup.player_distribution: Players are distributed evenly among the four outer corner stations. Two balls start at opposite corners (A and C) to maximize technical volume and force players to scan before receiving.

session_practices.explanation: Player A plays a firm, low pass diagonally into the central box for Player B, who has checked away from their corner. Player B must receive the ball inside the yellow cone box, execute a quick turn (e.g., inside hook, outside flick, or Cruyff turn), and open up their body to play a pass out to Player C. Players follow their pass to rotate to the next station.
coaching_points: Passes into the central box must be hit with pace to mimic breaking an opponent's midfield line.
The receiving player must check their shoulder to scan behind them before entering the central box.
The first touch inside the box must be soft and directional, turning the ball smoothly into the path of the next passing target.

session_practices.name: 3v1 to 3v2 Rondo: Overload and Penetrate

pitch_diagram.image_description: A rectangular 30x15 meter grid divided cleanly into two equal 15x15 meter halves by a dashed center line. Two small mini-gates made of cones sit on each deep baseline. A 3v1 overload is established in the initial possession grid.

tactical_layout.dimensions: 30m x 15m total grid split into two 15m x 15m zones
tactical_layout.equipment: 4 mini cone gates on the endlines
tactical_layout.player_alignment: Blue group attacking with a numerical advantage in the active half against an orange defender

setup.player_roles: 3 attacking players (blue) and 1 defending player (orange) occupy the active half. 1 resting defender stands inside the opposite half ready to engage when the ball crosses the line.

session_practices.explanation: The 3 blue attackers circulate the ball inside their half, attempting to keep possession against the 1 orange defender. After completing a sequence of 4 passes, they look for an opening to pass through the mini-gates or dribble across the center line. As soon as the ball enters the opposite half, the resting defender becomes active, and 1 supporting attacker joins to create a live 3v2 matching block. If defenders win the ball, they score by passing back across the center line.
